name: Walk and Turn
summary: Walk a single cursor through the grid, turning clockwise whenever the next cell would leave the grid or has already been visited.
visualizationIntent: init: draw the matrix as a grid with an empty 'seen' overlay and an empty spiral-order list; the cursor sits at the top-left. loop: a single cursor walks the grid one cell per step, tinting the current cell and appending its value to the spiral list; visited cells stay lit. check: before each move the cursor probes the next cell; if it is off the grid or already visited it turns clockwise. move: the cursor glides to the next cell. return: the spiral-order list holds every element and every grid cell is lit as visited.

lineExplanations.3: Create the list that will hold the elements in spiral order.
lineExplanations.4: Count the rows of the matrix.
lineExplanations.5: Count the columns of the matrix.
lineExplanations.6: Prepare a grid of 'seen' flags to remember which cells were visited.
lineExplanations.7: Build one flag row for every matrix row.
lineExplanations.8: Start that flag row as all-false (nothing visited yet).
lineExplanations.9: Add the flag row to the seen grid.
lineExplanations.10: List the four clockwise directions: right, down, left, up.
lineExplanations.11: Start the cursor on the top row.
lineExplanations.12: Start the cursor on the left column.
lineExplanations.14: Take exactly m*n steps — one per cell.
lineExplanations.15: Record the value at the current cell.
lineExplanations.16: Mark the current cell as visited.
lineExplanations.17: Compute the row we would move to if we keep going straight.
lineExplanations.18: Compute the column we would move to if we keep going straight.
lineExplanations.19: Test whether that next cell would leave the grid.
lineExplanations.20: If the next cell is off the grid or already visited, we must turn.
lineExplanations.21: Turn clockwise to the next direction, wrapping around after 'up'.
lineExplanations.22: Recompute the next row after turning.
lineExplanations.23: Recompute the next column after turning.
lineExplanations.24: Move the cursor to the chosen next row.
lineExplanations.25: Move the cursor to the chosen next column.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def spiralOrder(self, matrix): receives the 2-D matrix.
syntaxExplanations.4: len(matrix) is the number of rows.
syntaxExplanations.5: len(matrix[0]) is the number of columns.
syntaxExplanations.6: seen = [] starts an empty list of flag rows.
syntaxExplanations.7: for ri in range(m): loops over each matrix row.
syntaxExplanations.8: [False] * n makes a row of n False flags.
syntaxExplanations.10: directions holds [row-step, col-step] pairs for right, down, left, up.
syntaxExplanations.11: row = 0 puts the cursor on the top row.
syntaxExplanations.12: col = 0 puts the cursor on the left column.
syntaxExplanations.14: for step in range(m * n): one iteration per cell.
syntaxExplanations.15: matrix[row][col] reads the current value; append adds it.
syntaxExplanations.16: seen[row][col] = True marks the cell visited.
syntaxExplanations.17: row + directions[d][0] is the next row in the current direction.
syntaxExplanations.18: col + directions[d][1] is the next column in the current direction.
syntaxExplanations.19: the comparisons with 'or' test whether the next cell is outside the grid.
syntaxExplanations.20: if out_of_bounds or seen[...]: 'or' short-circuits so seen is only read when in bounds.
syntaxExplanations.21: (d + 1) % 4 rotates to the next direction and wraps from 3 back to 0.
syntaxExplanations.22: recompute next_row for the new direction.
syntaxExplanations.23: recompute next_col for the new direction.
syntaxExplanations.24: row = next_row commits the vertical move.
syntaxExplanations.25: col = next_col commits the horizontal move.
syntaxExplanations.26: return result hands back the spiral order.
